method: 3 lenses (transport-correctness, gating-wiring, regression-scope) + skeptic verify + synthesis; 18 agents

verdict.byteIdenticalContract: intact — mesh.enable=false default; NO_MESH ANDed into STANDALONE_REDIS_BROKER; every confirmed defect is mesh-gated
verdict.gatedBy: 2 confirmed MAJORs breaking FOLDED RC4a must-fix contracts inside mesh mode

mustFix_folded_before_cut.title: Membership ignored heartbeat liveness — violated the MeshNodeDirectory 'live-by-heartbeat ∩ has-address' SPI contract
mustFix_folded_before_cut.detail: Broker had no heartbeat view and the directory did not intersect, so a crashed peer whose mesh address had not yet TTL-expired (~10-30s window) counted toward shouldReach; a healthy sole-survivor false-degraded, and under on-redis-loss=CLOSE_ALL force-closed every local WS client (code 1011).
mustFix_folded_before_cut.fix: MeshBroker.setDeadNodeView(Supplier<Set<String>>) wired by auto-config to heartbeat.findExpiredNodes(timeoutMs); membershipTick subtracts dead peers before computing shouldReach; evaluateReachability restores ACTIVE when shouldReach==0 (no sticky DEGRADED for a lone node). Tests: MeshDegradeTest heartbeatDeadPeer_isNotAMembershipObligation_noFalseDegrade + isolationClears_whenNoLivePeersRemain_restores.

mustFix_folded_before_cut.title: connect-timeout-ms config never wired — connectionTo blocked the caller (WebSocket handler) thread on cf.sync() with Netty's default 30s timeout
mustFix_folded_before_cut.detail: ClusterProperties.Mesh.connectTimeoutMs was read into the bean but never reached the client bootstrap; ChannelOption.CONNECT_TIMEOUT_MILLIS was unset. A dead/black-holing peer (crashed mid-advertise-TTL) could stall the broadcast hot path ~30s, serially per peer. Contradicts the M1/§4 hot-path-safety intent.
mustFix_folded_before_cut.fix: MeshBroker takes connectTimeoutMs; start() sets ChannelOption.CONNECT_TIMEOUT_MILLIS; configure passes m.getConnectTimeoutMs(). Test: MeshConnectTimeoutTest asserts the bootstrap option.

alsoFolded_bonus.severity: MINOR (skeptic-downgraded from MAJOR; self-healing but a real fd leak)
alsoFolded_bonus.title: Stale outbound-channel entry orphaned the fresh connection under a flapping peer
alsoFolded_bonus.detail: connectionTo used putIfAbsent, which returned a dead-but-still-mapped prev WITHOUT storing the freshly dialed channel — orphaning a live connection and re-dialing per send until the dead entry's async closeFuture-remove fired.
alsoFolded_bonus.fix: putIfAbsent/remove replace loop so the fresh channel is always cached (or a genuinely live prev returned). Test: MeshBrokerTest connectionTo_replacesDeadCachedChannel_noLeak.

backlog_deferred_to_RC4c_or_RC4d.title: mesh.enable=true silently ignored when redis.cluster-nodes or nats.servers set — a different transport wins with no warn/fail-fast
backlog_deferred_to_RC4c_or_RC4d.fix: symmetric @ConditionalOnExpression fail-fast guard (mirror natsTransportClasspathGuard) before GA

backlog_deferred_to_RC4c_or_RC4d.title: idle-timeout-ms is a published non-zero-default knob but no IdleStateHandler is installed — inert in RC4a
backlog_deferred_to_RC4c_or_RC4d.fix: wire IdleStateHandler (RC4c) or mark no-effect-in-RC4a in config docs (done in release notes)

backlog_deferred_to_RC4c_or_RC4d.title: membership tick may fire isolation-degrade before ClusterMessageSender wires the transport listener (narrow non-default low advertise-ttl-ms precondition; worst case prevented by the ACTIVE&&ACTIVE publish gate; same pattern pre-exists in RedisPubSubBroker)
backlog_deferred_to_RC4c_or_RC4d.fix: reconcile broker.state() after wiring the listener (RC4c)

backlog_deferred_to_RC4c_or_RC4d.title: publish()/unicast() do a synchronous uncached Redis SCAN+GET on the hot path per message
backlog_deferred_to_RC4c_or_RC4d.detail: latency/scalability robustness regression vs RedisPubSubBroker's fire-and-forget; delivery still works, at-most-once holds, 2-node E2E passes; not a correctness/contract break
backlog_deferred_to_RC4c_or_RC4d.fix: cache the peer-address snapshot from membershipTick; publish/unicast read the volatile snapshot (RC4c)

backlog_deferred_to_RC4c_or_RC4d.title: mesh counters (incl. M1 mesh.send_dropped_backpressure) write to a throwaway ClusterRuntimeStats the meter binder/health indicator never read
backlog_deferred_to_RC4c_or_RC4d.detail: latent until RC4d adds netty.cluster.mesh.* meters; the M1 drop BEHAVIOR is correct, only its observability is mis-wired
backlog_deferred_to_RC4c_or_RC4d.fix: inject sender.getClusterRuntimeStats() into the MeshBroker (RC4d, when folding mesh meters)
